channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-19
item.description:
Our open-source research community has seen significant progress across multiple projects. Notably, the `giladfeldman/docpluck` project has shipped version 2.4.92, with updates to documentation and rendering features. Meanwhile, `Davidvandijcke/coarse` has made substantial strides in internationalization, with fixes and features added to support multiple languages, and is preparing for a new release, version 1.8.0. The `JamieCummins/regcheck` project has also seen improvements, with error message enhancements, bug fixes, and code optimizations on its staging branch. These updates demonstrate the connections and collaborations within our community, as projects like `coarse` work towards more inclusive and accessible tools, while `docpluck` and `regcheck` focus on refining their core functionalities. Overall, it's an exciting time for our community, with many projects pushing forward and making notable advancements.

123 commits across 7 active repositories this week.

New releases: metacheck v0.1.0-beta

Dormant repos revived: output-it-forward

Most active:

regcheck33 commits
docpluck32 commits
coarse31 commits
metacheck24 commits
escicheck1 commits

item.title: metacheck: 5 commits today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

The 'main' branch was updated to fix issue #314 by updating the metacheck page. The 'dev' branch saw a change in the Get Started vignette, where 'create_report_app()' was fixed to 'report_app()'. The 'ethics_check' branch had an 'ethics_check' added along with a bug fix. The 'new_modules' branch initially had a fix for app instruction #314 reverted, but this revert was then itself reverted, effectively fixing the app instruction #314.

5 commit(s) today:

  • 25c15a6 [main] update metacheck page to fix #314 — Lakens
  • 73736b0 [dev] Fix create_report_app() to report_app() in Get Started vignette — Daniel Lakens
  • 00d7953 [ethics_check] add ethics_check + bug fix — Lakens
  • 2b8a367 [new_modules] Revert "fix app instruction #314" — Lakens
  • 1d3719b [new_modules] fix app instruction #314 — Lakens
  • dccb9f4 [main] update module names in vignettes — Lakens
  • 684ccdf [main] Update DESCRIPTION fix #313 — Daniel Lakens
  • 7d7127d [new_modules] fix #313 — Lakens
  • 9749095 [new_modules] explore ideas for z-curve and grim — Lakens
  • a7dea29 [main] Merge pull request #312 from scienceverse/dev — Lisa DeBruine
  • f09fb47 [dev] zenodo citation — Lisa DeBruine
  • 00eaa3b [main] Merge pull request #311 from scienceverse/dev — Lisa DeBruine
  • 767da17 [dev] update apps and docs — Lisa DeBruine
  • 9d012e7 [dev] Merge pull request #310 from nielsvanberkel/main — Lisa DeBruine
  • 8a605a3 [dev] Merge branch 'dev' into main — Lisa DeBruine
  • 3e127fa [dev] Replace devtools::install_github with pak::pkg_install — nielsvanberkel
  • 9ef4d94 [prereg_regcheck] name fix — Lakens
  • 2b0b4e4 [prereg_regcheck] working version of Regcheck — Lakens
  • 9a007d9 [dev] TUE server uses 0.9.0 GROBID now as well — Lakens
  • 920c2d9 [dev] Add vignette describing all references — Lakens

View all commits →

item.title: docpluck: 3 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

The `main` branch was the focus of the recent work, with multiple commits made to it. The commit "docs(iterate): 2026-06-18 run handoff (v2.4.92 shipped, caption-guard reverted, paused at architectural boundary)" indicates that a handoff was run, version 2.4.92 was shipped, the caption guard was reverted, and work was paused at an architectural boundary. Another commit on the `main` branch, "docs(iterate): cycle 2 (v2.4.92 affiliation guard) + cycle 3 (caption-follows REVERTED) journal", added documentation for cycle 2, which includes the affiliation guard for version 2.4.92, and cycle 3, where the caption-follows feature was reverted. A feature commit "feat(render): never promote affiliation lines to subsection headings (v2.4.92)" changed the rendering to never promote affiliation lines to subsection headings, specifically for version 2.4.92.

3 commit(s) today:

  • 2fd8711 [main] docs(iterate): 2026-06-18 run handoff (v2.4.92 shipped, caption-guard reverted, paused at architectural boundary) — Gilad Feldman
  • c671929 [main] docs(iterate): cycle 2 (v2.4.92 affiliation guard) + cycle 3 (caption-follows REVERTED) journal — Gilad Feldman
  • cf420ca [main] feat(render): never promote affiliation lines to subsection headings (v2.4.92) — Gilad Feldman
  • f392957 [main] docs(iterate): v2.4.91 shipped handoff + cycle journal — Gilad Feldman
  • 7edc62f [main] chore(canary): onboard efendic/maier/xiao into rotating pool (rotation now active) — Gilad Feldman
  • 662c4df [main] @ — Gilad Feldman
  • cfe86f7 [main] feat: add optional extraction guardrails for size and timeout — Gilad Feldman
  • aa82a25 [main] docs(todo): queue investigation for items 3 and 8 — Gilad Feldman
  • 617db41 [main] refactor: surface fallback exception classes in method metadata — Gilad Feldman
  • 24aa793 [main] feat: add fallback telemetry counters and optional logging — Gilad Feldman
  • a5da768 [main] ci: add docs-code consistency guard — Gilad Feldman
  • f8594c3 [main] docs: sync normalization and benchmark claims with current code — Gilad Feldman
  • c8800bd [main] docs: add root README landing page — Gilad Feldman
  • 4c55d6e [main] docs(iterate): 2026-06-16 resume handoff + concurrency-collision lesson — Gilad Feldman
  • 436fc5a [main] refactor(extract_columns): remove orphaned duplicate band re-extractor — Gilad Feldman
  • f7c3729 [main] docs(handoff): RC-1 Step 2 continuation handoff (post-deploy) + canary case-norm todo — Gilad Feldman
  • 818c3d0 [main] docs(iterate): cycle-2 learnings — RC-1 Step 2 + word-preservation-guard-is-the-safety — Gilad Feldman
  • da6c90f [main] docs(iterate): handoff — RC-1 Step 2 first cut shipped (v2.4.90, ship-dark) — Gilad Feldman
  • 1325d14 [main] feat(v2.4.90): RC-1 Step 2 -- per-band region-aware two-column re-extraction (ship-dark) — Gilad Feldman
  • 5782c6c [main] docs(iterate): correct handoff deploy-state row — main push HELD (local only) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: coarse: 17 commits today
item.description:
Davidvandijcke/coarse

Work was done on the `main`, `dev`, `feat/i18n-setup-compare`, `feat/i18n-status-review-pages`, `feat/237-lang-eval`, `feat/nl-flemish`, `feat/229-i18n-labels`, `feat/231-site-i18n`, and `feat/226-i18n-deenglish` branches. The `dev` branch had a fix for internationalization, specifically to "localize confirmation email + review page follows review language" as stated in the commit message. The `dev` branch also had a fix to "enable RLS on schema_migrations bookkeeping table". Additionally, the `feat/i18n-setup-compare` branch added a feature for internationalization, which included localizing setup and compare pages to follow the site language, as described in the commit message "feat(i18n): localize setup + compare pages; follow site language". The `dev` branch also included a new feature to add "GLM 5.2 to the model picker" and had a "chore(release): v1.8.0" commit, indicating the preparation of a new release.

17 commit(s) today:

  • 05aad76 [main] Merge pull request #245 from Davidvandijcke/dev — David Van Dijcke
  • 49a72b3 [dev] Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/main' into sync/reconcile-v1.8.0 — Davidvandijcke
  • 2e86c5f [dev] fix(i18n): localize confirmation email + review page follows review language (#244) — David Van Dijcke
  • 907c629 [dev] Merge pull request #242 from Davidvandijcke/release/v1.8.0 — David Van Dijcke
  • f54b584 [dev] fix(deploy): enable RLS on schema_migrations bookkeeping table (#243) — David Van Dijcke

View all commits →

item.title: regcheck: 7 commits today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

The staging branch was the focus of the recent work, where the error message was "improve[d]" as per the commit message. The osf bug and the oss bug were specifically "fix[ed]" in separate commits. The codebase also saw the removal of "groq endpoints" and the json handling was "harden[ed]". Additionally, the storage was modified to be "more efficient" and the oss SDK handler was "patch[ed]". All of these changes were made by Jamie Cummins on the staging branch.

7 commit(s) today:

  • 7fb80fe [staging] improve error message — Jamie Cummins
  • a2f7302 [staging] fix osf bug — Jamie Cummins
  • d2089d5 [staging] remove groq endpoints — Jamie Cummins
  • 51b9ba6 [staging] harden json handling — Jamie Cummins
  • 6cdde08 [staging] fix oss bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 1fd2de7 [staging] use more efficient storage — Jamie Cummins
  • 4b7a527 [staging] patch oss SDK handler — Jamie Cummins
  • e514303 [staging] fix oss handling bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 599489b [staging] harden qwen handling — Jamie Cummins
  • 8e7e452 [staging] improve embedding efficiency, purge dead code — Jamie Cummins
  • 88e039c [staging] replace deprecated llama — Jamie Cummins
  • 80dcf34 [staging] harden osf handling again — Jamie Cummins
  • d3471fe [staging] change llama model to best available — Jamie Cummins
  • 65d8ba6 [staging] production hardening - staging test — Jamie Cummins
  • 9c4cd96 [staging] prod preparation: phase 1 + 2 — Jamie Cummins
  • 57b6bc1 [staging] readd reasoning effort for oss — Jamie Cummins
  • 9735187 [staging] harden osf handling — Jamie Cummins
  • 8cddeb8 [staging] add html, osf support — Jamie Cummins
  • 978ec81 [staging] Update privacy.html — Jamie Cummins
  • 50f52d9 [staging] frontend updates - light mode! — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →

item.title: escicheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

The main branch was worked on in this set of commits. The commit message indicates that the repository was "sync from escicheckapp@f52d7ce (effectcheck v0.6.3)" which suggests that changes from the escicheckapp repository, specifically from the commit f52d7ce, were synchronized into the main branch, bringing it up to date with effectcheck version 0.6.3. This sync is associated with issue #17. No other branches were mentioned in the provided commits. The synchronization brought the main branch in line with the specified version of effectcheck.

1 commit(s) today:

  • 4be3331 [main] sync from escicheckapp@f52d7ce (effectcheck v0.6.3) (#17) — Gilad Feldman
  • 1f1a89a [main] sync from escicheckapp@e060e98 (effectcheck v0.6.2) (#16) — Gilad Feldman
  • f7e25f6 [sync/20260525-150441-b801333] sync from escicheckapp@b801333 (effectcheck v0.6.2) — github-actions[bot]
  • d0db89d [main] sync from escicheckapp@5093197 (effectcheck v0.6.1) (#14) — Gilad Feldman
  • a44f236 [main] sync from escicheckapp@1ebe05f (effectcheck v0.5.18) (#13) — Gilad Feldman
  • 8193b8f [main] sync from escicheckapp@bffa468 (effectcheck v0.5.11) (#12) — Gilad Feldman
  • 3077a57 [main] sync from escicheckapp@82d266e (effectcheck v0.5.11) (#11) — Gilad Feldman
  • 7f365d7 [main] sync from escicheckapp@643e05d (effectcheck v0.5.11) (#10) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: output-it-forward: activity update
item.description:
giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward

The `main` branch was worked on, where a Chrome Webstore link was added for OIF, as stated in the commit message "Add Chrome Webstore link for OIF". No other branches were modified in this set of commits. The addition of the Chrome Webstore link is the sole change made in this update. There are no new features, fixes, or modules added aside from this link. The commit message directly mentions the addition of this link, indicating its purpose. No other code modifications were included in this commit.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
